headline: El Salvador Eliminates Trachoma as Public Health Threat
meta_description: El Salvador has been validated by WHO as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem, marking a significant victory in global eye health.
summary_short: El Salvador has achieved a major health milestone, officially eliminating trachoma as a public health problem according to WHO validation.
body:
El Salvador has reached a significant milestone in public health: the World Health Organization has validated the country as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem.

Trachoma, an infectious eye disease caused by bacteria, has long been a leading preventable cause of blindness worldwide. The disease thrives in areas with limited access to clean water and sanitation, making its elimination a marker of broader health system improvements.

El Salvador's achievement represents years of sustained effort to improve health conditions and access to treatment across the country. The WHO validation confirms that the nation has successfully reduced trachoma to levels where it no longer poses a significant threat to public health.

This recognition joins a growing list of countries addressing preventable blindness, offering hope that other nations facing similar challenges can follow similar paths to elimination.
